---
title: "MCP & Connecting AI to Data & Tools"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} · **Technical & Agentic** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Technical & Agentic track](technical-agentic.qmd){.track-back}

*Understand how assistants connect to real systems — and the access risks that come with it.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Explain what MCP (Model Context Protocol) does
- Recognize when connecting AI to a tool helps vs. adds risk
- Apply least-privilege and approval before connecting to data

## Overview

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard way to connect an assistant to outside data sources and tools — a file store, a database, a calendar. It's the plumbing behind agents that do more than chat. Powerful, and a new surface for mistakes and access risk.

Connect with least privilege: give the narrowest access that does the job, prefer read-only, and confirm Drexel's rules before connecting to any real or sensitive system.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Advanced — Map a connection with least privilege</summary>
```

**Time** ~15 min · **Tools** conceptual + the connector’s docs

**Goal.** Plan a safe AI-to-tool connection.

**Setup.** Pick a task that would need AI to reach a tool or data source (e.g., read a shared folder of public reports).

**Steps.**

1. Write exactly what access the task needs — then the narrowest version that still works (read-only? one folder?).

2. Get a second opinion:

   > Here’s the task and the access I’m considering. What’s the least-privilege version, and what could go wrong if it’s over-scoped? [task]

3. Decide read-only vs. write, and what stays off-limits.

**Expected result.** A least-privilege access plan.

**Check your work.** Could a misbehaving connection do damage with this access? If so, narrow it.

**Common pitfalls.** Convenience tempts over-permissioning. Prefer read-only; confirm Drexel rules before connecting any real or sensitive system.

**Stretch (optional).** Decide whether the connection is even worth the risk versus a manual copy-paste.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Advanced — Risk-check the connection</summary>
```

**Time** ~12 min · **Tools** conceptual

**Goal.** Put a human approval step before consequential actions.

**Setup.** Your plan from Activity 1.

**Steps.**

1. List what could go wrong if the connection misbehaves or the AI is misled by content it reads.

2. Add a human-approval step before any write, send, or delete action.

3. Find the worst case:

   > Given this connection, what’s the single highest-risk action, and how do I gate it?

**Expected result.** A risk list plus an approval gate on the riskiest action.

**Check your work.** Is there any path where the AI acts irreversibly without a human? Close it.

**Common pitfalls.** Connected agents can be misled by malicious content (prompt injection) — keep humans on consequential steps.

**Stretch (optional).** Note which actions you’d never connect to AI at all.

```{=html}
</details>
```


## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "MCP is, in plain terms…", "options": ["A standard way to connect AI to data and tools", "A chatbot", "A statistic", "A file format"], "answer": 0, "explain": "It's the connection layer for agents."}, {"q": "The right access posture is…", "options": ["Least privilege — narrowest access, prefer read-only", "Full admin", "Whatever's easiest", "No limits"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Minimize the blast radius."}, {"q": "Before connecting AI to a real system you should…", "options": ["Confirm Drexel's data rules and approvals", "Just connect it", "Disable logging", "Share credentials"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Approval and data classification come first."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

Available in the shared OneDrive folder **Staff Faculty AI Workshop → Readings**, and online where linked:

- [**How Agents Use Systems Differently (Davis Treybig)**](https://davistreybig.substack.com/p/how-agents-use-systems-differently) — what changes when AI connects to systems.
- [**A Practical Guide to Building Agents (OpenAI)**](https://cdn.openai.com/business-guides-and-resources/a-practical-guide-to-building-agents.pdf) — tools and connections in agent design.

## Useful resources

- [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io) — the open MCP standard.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Connect with **least privilege**; get approval before any connection to real or sensitive data. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
